codeReview.summary: Both validator agent definition files are well-structured, complete, and satisfy all four validation contract assertions (VAL-VALID-001, VAL-VALID-002, VAL-VALID-006, VAL-VALID-008). Frontmatter is correct with valid names, kind: local, all valid Gemini CLI tool names, appropriate model/temperature/max_turns/timeout_mins values. The procedure bodies are comprehensive with clear phased instructions covering all required behaviors. No blocking issues found.

issues.description: The example synthesis report JSON hardcodes "milestone": "validation" rather than using a placeholder like "<milestone>". Since the agent is milestone-generic, a placeholder would be more instructive. Minor: unlikely to cause real issues since the body text repeatedly uses the <milestone> placeholder pattern elsewhere.

Same as scrutiny-validator: the example synthesis JSON hardcodes "milestone": "validation" rather than "<milestone>". Consistent with the other agent, so not an inconsistency, but a minor documentation clarity issue.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker was assigned skillName 'extension-builder' but the Skill tool returned 'Skill extension-builder not found'. The worker deviated and completed the feature manually. The skill file exists at .factory/skills/extension-builder/SKILL.md, so the skill is available in the project's Factory config, but was not loadable via the Skill tool in the worker's runtime. This is documented in the handoff's skillFeedback.deviations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows: Tool: Skill { skill: 'extension-builder' } returned not-found. Handoff skillFeedback.followedProcedure: false, deviations[0]: 'The required extension-builder skill was not available in the environment'.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker also attempted to invoke a 'mission-worker-base' skill (visible in transcript skeleton) which also was not found. The extension-builder skill references 'worker-base' for startup/cleanup handling. This skill loading failure pattern is recurring.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton shows first tool call: Skill { skill: 'mission-worker-base' } — this skill doesn't appear to exist in .factory/skills/.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The .factory/services.yaml only defines validation commands (validate-json, validate-extension, validate-toml, validate-shell) — no test, typecheck, or lint commands. The worker correctly identified this gap. When the scrutiny-validator agent runs against this project, it will find no test/typecheck/lint commands in .mission/services.yaml and will need to handle this correctly (the agent body does instruct recording missing commands as blocking gaps). This is by design for a declarative extension project but worth noting.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/services.yaml contents: commands: validate-json, validate-extension, validate-toml, validate-shell. No 'test', 'typecheck', or 'lint' entries.

summary: Feature passes review. Both agents/scrutiny-validator.md and agents/user-testing-validator.md are well-implemented with correct YAML frontmatter (name, kind: local, valid tool names, model: gemini-2.5-pro, appropriate temperature/max_turns/timeout_mins), comprehensive multi-phase procedures, correct synthesis report paths (.mission/validation/<milestone>/scrutiny/synthesis.json and .mission/validation/<milestone>/user-testing/synthesis.json), re-run logic that checks previous synthesis reports and scopes re-validation to failures only, and proper structured handoff protocols. All four assigned validation contract assertions (VAL-VALID-001, VAL-VALID-002, VAL-VALID-006, VAL-VALID-008) are satisfied. Only non-blocking issues found: minor hardcoded milestone name in example JSON snippets.
